compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic collapse of biological material. The registry-defined specimens are no longer visible as healthy plants.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss (Anchor rabbit present, plant material absent)

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no vegetative growth detected.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty soil surface; no vegetative growth detected.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: White rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos specimen has been removed or completely decomposed.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: Empty soil surface; no vegetative growth detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears desiccated and dark; no signs of moisture or fungal activity.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in the central black pot; no other organic matter.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no biological debris or secondary growth.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows minimal plant presence; P3 has a small leaf, P4 has a single stem.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Progressive loss of structural integrity; the stem in P4 appears to wither and lose turgor.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Total disappearance of all vegetative matter. The biome is now effectively a collection of empty pots.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The transition from a sparse, struggling state to a completely empty state indicates either total plant mortality followed by decomposition or manual removal of the specimens.

anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments as a non-botanical intervention.
Complete absence of expected flora across all four pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a complete systemic failure of the indoor biome. Over the observed five-day period, the specimens (P1-P4) have transitioned from a state of extreme neglect/minimal growth to total absence. The white rabbit anchor in P3 remains, but the Pothos has vanished. The soil in all containers is devoid of life, suggesting the environment is no longer supporting plant growth.
